snippet.title: Leadership in Challenging Times: A Discussion with Jim Fitterling, Dow
snippet.description:
"Leadership is about being the number-one change agent. You have to set the bar higher. You have to define what's possible for the organization…But…you've got to find the right balance. You can't just solve for one thing at the expense of everything else. Our job is to figure out how to do better, but we also have to be affordable, and we have to be sustainable long term." 

This reflection comes from Jim Fitterling, Chair and CEO of Dow. Fitterling is the latest guest in CED’s Leadership in Challenging Times podcast/webcast series and—along with the Dow team—a recipient of CED’s 2023 Distinguished Leadership Awards. 

CED’s Leadership in Challenging Times series interviews outstanding business leaders who are the recipients of CED's Annual Distinguished Leadership Awards, about the unprecedented challenges facing our nation and how they have helped chart a path forward for their companies, communities, and the nation at large.

snippet.title: Stock DOWN 25% Since the IPO! 😱 Dividend Yield AT 4%! A Stock to BUY NOW or WAIT for further PAIN?!🤔
snippet.description:
This stock IPO'd earlier this year.  In addition, they were from a spin-off from a major dividend growth stock.  Lastly, the dividend yield is soaring to 4% with plenty of iconic brands under their belt.

Why do dividends matter?  Dividends are one of the best passive income sources.  Dividend investing increases your passive income, as you reach financial freedom!

Reference items:
►SoFi - Earn $25 FREE Stock when you Invest $10!: https://bit.ly/3BHfJfs
►SoFi  - Open a Savings/Checking Account, earn up to $275!: https://rb.gy/3itol
►Fundrise - Dividend Diplomats Use this for Real Estate Investing: https://fundrise.sjv.io/9aJWW
►GoogleFi - Save ~$30 month for switching to Google's Phone Plan: https://g.co/fi/r/1292VY
►Dividend Diplomats Stock Portfolios: https://bit.ly/3fc7G2B
►Dividend Diplomats Blog: https://www.dividenddiplomats.com/

This dividend stock is now down 25% from the initial public offering (IPO) price.

Further, the "big" discount was a 7% off on the stock price over the weekend of August 18th, if you owned shares of JNJ, to own this stock - by trading in some JNJ shares, in a tax-free transaction.

The stock was trading near $23 at that time and closed slightly higher at $24 per share after that weekend.

The stock has now plummeted down to almost a flat $20 per share, over a double digit decline.

Now, the dividend yield is up to almost 4%.

The brands are deep and have loyalty from their consumer base.  Brands such as: Tylenol, Band-aid, Aveeno, Lubriderm, Neutrogena, Benadryl, Sudafed, Zyrtec, you name it.

This dividend stock is Kenvue (KVUE).

The dividend stock metrics appear fairly solid, with ~62% dividend payout ratio and a less than 16x price to earnings ratio.

Question I have is - are you buying this dividend stock right now? Do you own Kenvue stock?  If you don't own this stock, do you think this is a stock to buy right now?

Thank you everyone!  Subscribe, like and let us know your feedback & questions below!

Follow us on:
►Twitter: https://twitter.com/DvdndDiplomats
►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dividenddiplomats/
►Threads: https://www.threads.net/@dividenddiplomats

#investing #stocks #dividends #financialfreedom #money #moneytips #PersonalFinance
